children.heading: Order of priority of participants and beneficiaries
children.label: a Order of priority of participants and beneficiaries

children.text: First, to that portion of each individual’s accrued benefit which is derived from the participant’s contributions to the plan which were not mandatory contributions.
Second, to that portion of each individual’s accrued benefit which is derived from the participant’s mandatory contributions.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of the benefit of a participant or beneficiary which was in pay status as of the beginning of the 3-year period ending on the termination date of the plan, to each such benefit, based on the provisions of the plan (as in effect during the 5-year period ending on such date) under which such benefit would be the least,

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a participant’s or beneficiary’s benefit (other than a benefit described in subparagraph (A)) which would have been in pay status as of the beginning of such 3-year period if the participant had retired prior to the beginning of the 3-year period and if his benefits had commenced (in the normal form of annuity under the plan) as of the beginning of such period, to each such benefit based on the provisions of the plan (as in effect during the 5-year period ending on such date) under which such benefit would be the least.

children.label: A
children.text: to all other benefits (if any) of individuals under the plan guaranteed under this subchapter (determined without regard to ), andsection 1322b(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: to the additional benefits (if any) which would be determined under subparagraph (A) if did not apply.section 1322(b)(5)(B) of this title
Fifth, to all other nonforfeitable benefits under the plan.
Sixth, to all other benefits under the plan.

children.heading: Adjustment of allocations; reallocations; mandatory contributions; establishment of subclasses and categories
children.label: b Adjustment of allocations; reallocations; mandatory contributions; establishment of subclasses and categories

children.text: The amount allocated under any paragraph of subsection (a) with respect to any benefit shall be properly adjusted for any allocation of assets with respect to that benefit under a prior paragraph of subsection (a).

If the assets available for allocation under any paragraph of subsection (a) (other than paragraphs (4), (5), and (6)) are insufficient to satisfy in full the benefits of all individuals which are described in that paragraph, the assets shall be allocated pro rata among such individuals on the basis of the present value (as of the termination date) of their respective benefits described in that paragraph.

If assets available for allocation under paragraph (4) of subsection (a) are insufficient to satisfy in full the benefits of all individuals who are described in that paragraph, the assets shall be allocated first to benefits described in subparagraph (A) of that paragraph. Any remaining assets shall then be allocated to benefits described in subparagraph (B) of that paragraph. If assets allocated to such subparagraph (B) are insufficient to satisfy in full the benefits described in that subparagraph, the assets shall be allocated pro rata among individuals on the basis of the present value (as of the termination date) of their respective benefits described in that subparagraph.

children.label: A
children.text: If this paragraph applies, except as provided in subparagraph (B), the assets shall be allocated to the benefits of individuals described in such paragraph (5) on the basis of the benefits of individuals which would have been described in such paragraph (5) under the plan as in effect at the beginning of the 5-year period ending on the date of plan termination.

children.label: B
children.text: If the assets available for allocation under subparagraph (A) are sufficient to satisfy in full the benefits described in such subparagraph (without regard to this subparagraph), then for purposes of subparagraph (A), benefits of individuals described in such subparagraph shall be determined on the basis of the plan as amended by the most recent plan amendment effective during such 5-year period under which the assets available for allocation are sufficient to satisfy in full the benefits of individuals described in subparagraph (A) and any assets remaining to be allocated under such subparagraph shall be allocated under subparagraph (A) on the basis of the plan as amended by the next succeeding plan amendment effective during such period.

If the Secretary of the Treasury determines that the allocation made pursuant to this section (without regard to this paragraph) results in discrimination prohibited by then, if required to prevent the disqualification of the plan (or any trust under the plan) under section 401(a) or 403(a) of title 26, the assets allocated under subsections (a)(4)(B), (a)(5), and (a)(6) shall be reallocated to the extent necessary to avoid such discrimination.section 401(a)(4) of title 26

The term “mandatory contributions” means amounts contributed to the plan by a participant which are required as a condition of employment, as a condition of participation in such plan, or as a condition of obtaining benefits under the plan attributable to employer contributions. For this purpose, the total amount of mandatory contributions of a participant is the amount of such contributions reduced (but not below zero) by the sum of the amounts paid or distributed to him under the plan before its termination.
A plan may establish subclasses and categories within the classes described in paragraphs (1) through (6) of subsection (a) in accordance with regulations prescribed by the corporation.

children.heading: Increase or decrease in value of assets
children.label: c Increase or decrease in value of assets
children.text: Any increase or decrease in the value of the assets of a single-employer plan occurring during the period beginning on the later of (1) the date a trustee is appointed under or (2) the date on which the plan is terminated is to be allocated between the plan and the corporation in the manner determined by the court (in the case of a court-appointed trustee) or as agreed upon by the corporation and the plan administrator in any other case. Any increase or decrease in the value of the assets of a single-employer plan occurring after the date on which the plan is terminated shall be credited to, or suffered by, the corporation.section 1342(b) of this title

children.heading: Distribution of residual assets; restrictions on reversions pursuant to recently amended plans; assets attributable to employee contributions; calculation of remaining assets
children.label: d Distribution of residual assets; restrictions on reversions pursuant to recently amended plans; assets attributable to employee contributions; calculation of remaining assets
A
children.text: all liabilities of the plan to participants and their beneficiaries have been satisfied,

children.label: B
children.text: the distribution does not contravene any provision of law, and

children.label: C
children.text: the plan provides for such a distribution in these circumstances.

children.label: A
children.text: In determining the extent to which a plan provides for the distribution of plan assets to the employer for purposes of paragraph (1)(C), any such provision, and any amendment increasing the amount which may be distributed to the employer, shall not be treated as effective before the end of the fifth calendar year following the date of the adoption of such provision or amendment.

children.label: B
children.text: A distribution to the employer from a plan shall not be treated as failing to satisfy the requirements of this paragraph if the plan has been in effect for fewer than 5 years and the plan has provided for such a distribution since the effective date of the plan.

children.label: C
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in regulations of the Secretary of the Treasury, in any case in which a transaction described in occurs, subparagraph (A) shall continue to apply separately with respect to the amount of any assets transferred in such transaction.section 1058 of this title

children.label: D
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “employer” includes any member of the controlled group of which the employer is a member. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the term “controlled group” means any group treated as a single employer under subsection (b), (c), (m) or () of .osection 414 of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: Before any distribution from a plan pursuant to paragraph (1), if any assets of the plan attributable to employee contributions remain after satisfaction of all liabilities described in subsection (a), such remaining assets shall be equitably distributed to the participants who made such contributions or their beneficiaries (including alternate payees, within the meaning of ).section 1056(d)(3)(K) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the market value of the total remaining assets, by

children.label: I
children.text: the numerator of which is the present value of all portions of the accrued benefits with respect to participants which are derived from participants’ mandatory contributions (referred to in subsection (a)(2)), and

children.label: II
children.text: the denominator of which is the present value of all benefits with respect to which assets are allocated under paragraphs (2) through (6) of subsection (a).

children.label: i
children.text: a participant under the plan, or

children.label: ii
children.text: an individual who has received, during the 3-year period ending with the termination date, a distribution from the plan of such individual’s entire nonforfeitable benefit in the form of a single sum distribution in accordance with or in the form of irrevocable commitments purchased by the plan from an insurer to provide such nonforfeitable benefit,section 1053(e) of this title

Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to limit the requirements of (as in effect immediately after the enactment of the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1990) or with respect to any distribution of residual assets of a single-employer plan to the employer.section 4980(d) of title 26section 1104(d) of this title

children.heading: Bankruptcy filing substituted for termination date
children.label: e Bankruptcy filing substituted for termination date
children.text: If a contributing sponsor of a plan has filed or has had filed against such person a petition seeking liquidation or reorganization in a case under title 11 or under any similar Federal law or law of a State or political subdivision, and the case has not been dismissed as of the termination date of the plan, then subsection (a)(3) shall be applied by treating the date such petition was filed as the termination date of the plan.

children.heading: Valuation of section 1362(c) liability for determining amounts payable by corporation to participants and beneficiaries
children.label: f Valuation of section 1362(c) liability for determining amounts payable by corporation to participants and beneficiaries
A
children.text: the amount of liability under as of the termination date of the plan, bysection 1362(c) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the applicable section 1362(c) recovery ratio.

children.label: i
children.text: the sum of the values of all recoveries under determined by the corporation in connection with plan terminations described under subparagraph (B), bears tosection 1362(c) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the sum of all the amounts of liability under with respect to such plans as of the termination date in connection with any such prior termination.section 1362(c) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the value of recoveries under have been determined by the corporation, andsection 1362(c) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: notices of intent to terminate were provided (or in the case of a termination by the corporation, a notice of determination under was issued) during the 5-Federal fiscal year period ending with the third fiscal year preceding the fiscal year in which occurs the date of the notice of intent to terminate (or the notice of determination under ) with respect to the plan termination for which the recovery ratio is being determined.section 1342 of this titlesection 1342 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the value of the recoveries on behalf of the plan under , tosection 1362(c) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of the liability owed under as of the date of plan termination to the trustee appointed under section 1342(b) or (c) of this title.section 1362(c) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: whether the amount of outstanding benefit liabilities exceeds $20,000,000, or

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of any liability under to the corporation or the trustee appointed under section 1342(b) or (c) of this title.section 1362 of this title

children.heading: Determinations
children.label: 4 Determinations
children.text: Determinations under this subsection shall be made by the corporation. Such determinations shall be binding unless shown by clear and convincing evidence to be unreasonable.
